int  line,

Throw a C pseudo-exception in debug mode

Parameters:
file The file were the exception was thrown
line The line were the exception was thrown
exc The exception number / type
desc A printf()-like formatted string holding a description of the thrown exception

char* cstrchrnul ( const char *  s,
int  c 
)

Replacement function for strchrnul() which is a GNU extension

Parameters:
s String to search for the character c
c The character to look for
Returns:
A pointer to the first instance of c or a pointer to the nul terminator if c is not found in the string

void dbg_error_internal ( const char *  filename,
int  lineno,
const char *  format,
  ... 
)

Prints a formatted string to the stderr stream, notifying the user of an error condition and aborts

Parameters:
filename The name of the source file in which this function was called
lineno The line where this function was called
format A format string using the printf() guidelines

Definition at line 62 of file util.c.

void dbg_printf_internal ( const char *  filename,
int  lineno,
const char *  format,
  ... 
)

Prints a formatted string to the stderr stream, the function prepends the filename and line where the function was called and appends a newline

Parameters:
filename The name of the file from which the function was called
lineno The number of the line in which the function was called
format A format string using the printf() guidelines

Definition at line 80 of file util.c.

void dbg_unreachable_internal ( const char *  filename,
int  lineno 
)

Aborts execution and emits a warning that the code has reached a point which shouldn't be reachable under normal conditions

Parameters:
filename The name of the file from which the function was called
lineno The number of the line in which the function was called

void dbg_warning_internal ( const char *  filename,
int  lineno,
const char *  format,
  ... 
)

Prints a formatted string to the stderr stream, warning the user about abnormal situations which do not prevent the program from continuing execution

Parameters:
filename The name of the source file in which this function was called
lineno The line where this function was called
format A format string using the printf() guidelines

struct timespec get_time_with_offset ( uint64_t  ms,
uint32_t  nanos 
) [read]

Returns a timespec structure filled with the current time plus the offset specified by ms and nanos

Parameters:
ms An offset in microseconds
nanos An offset in nanoseconds
Returns:
The current time plus the specified offset

void sort_asc_uint16_ptrs ( uint16_t *  array,
void **  ptrs,
uint32_t  len 
)

Sorts an uint16 array and a pointer array using the integer array as the reference. This function is used to sort the interface table

Parameters:
array A pointer to an uint16 array
ptrs A pointer to an array of pointers
len The length of the arrays
